metrics.narrative: Amy Snider named GEP's reliability as the core driver: 'there are constant bugs and glitches that we are dealing with every single week... the platform's just not stable enough at all' and confirmed reliability, not features, is the one thing that would need to be 'meaningfully better' to justify switching (Avoma discovery call, 14 Jan 2026). The 14 Jan 2026 scoping note also records that GEP is 'charging them a significant amount of $$$ to make changes and receive customized support,' a real but unquantified cost signal (Next_Step_History__c, 14 Jan 2026). No buyer-stated dollar figure, headcount, or cycle-time number has been captured; quantifying the cost of GEP's fees and the manual-PO/renewal-tracking overhead is the open move.

sources: Avoma Procurement Pinnacle reference, Next_Step_History__c 19 Feb 2026
champion.narrative: Amy Snider is Zycus's named champion (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c) and has demonstrably advocated: she drove the internal process across four department demos in April, personally attended Zycus's Procurement Pinnacle event (19 Feb 2026), and emailed on 11 May 2026 that she was 'confident in the demos... seen thus far.' What is missing is any confirmed contact from her since that 11 May email - seven rep touches through 6 Jul 2026 (Next_Step_History__c) have gone unanswered - so her current engagement has cooled from active to unresponsive, and it is unknown whether she retains influence with the incoming CEO.

competition.narrative: BitSight ran a structured downselection from an initial five-name field (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba per Competitors__c) to a final three as of 9 Mar 2026, with Zycus surviving the cut (Next_Step_History__c). GEP Worldwide, the incumbent, is the only competitor with fresh evidence: it is the explicit foil in the rep's most recent next step (6 Jul 2026, 'differentiate on service/support') and the subject of extensive discovery-call pain around reliability and customization cost. The identities of the other two current finalists are unconfirmed. The wedge is native NetSuite integration plus reliability and Merlin-driven UX (Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c), positioned against the real risk that 'safer' incumbency inertia wins by default while the deal sits idle.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is extensively documented and owned by Amy Snider and Bernardo Silva (Accounts Payable): weekly GEP support tickets for 'something silly that has changed from one day to the next,' no centralized renewal dashboard forcing manual Excel tracking of ~15 renewals a month, no shared purchasing-user visibility (Amy and Bernardo must each be individually tagged on contracts), fully manual PO creation after contract execution, and no visibility into remaining PO balance after partial invoicing (Avoma discovery call, 14 Jan 2026). Amy also confirmed she is 'not married to anything' and open to full process change, indicating genuine appetite to fix rather than just explore.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, legal, or InfoSec activity has started. On the 14 Jan 2026 discovery call, BitSight's GRC/security review of Zycus was explicitly deferred: 'I would definitely like to show...to the GRC team...down the road when that team is involved' - it has not resurfaced since. Current_Contract_Expiration__c (the GEP contract end date, a potential forcing function) is blank. Visibility here would come from asking Amy directly what BitSight's procurement/legal contracting path and typical cycle time look like once a vendor is chosen.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified on this deal at any point in six months (Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c = false, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c blank). Amy Snider (Global Director of Procurement) has run pricing, demo scheduling, and downselection logistics, but nothing in any call or field shows her controlling budget sign-off. James Hillier is a known BitSight contact at account level but has never appeared on a call or task tied to this opportunity - he is an unconfirmed candidate, not a mapped EB. The new CEO onboarding (Next_Step__c, 6 Jul 2026) now sits squarely on top of this gap: budget authority for the deal may rest with someone Zycus has never met.

decision_process.narrative: The call/next-step sequence maps the process clearly: intro (7 Jan) -> scoping + buy-in demo (14/16 Jan) -> vendor pool narrowed to top 5 with prelim pricing (17-23 Feb, Amy attended Zycus's Procurement Pinnacle event 19 Feb) -> shortlisted to 3 finalists with RFP issued (9 Mar, due 20 Mar) -> RFP submitted (18-30 Mar) -> four department demos: Legal (16 Apr), IT (21 Apr), AP/Finance (22 Apr) -> results expected 'this week' as of 4 and 11 May, then the process went silent. No Multiple_approvals__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, or Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c data exists, and the new CEO transition (6 Jul) has introduced an unmapped approval layer on top of an already-undocumented chain.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from two transcribed calls: a central contract-renewal dashboard (replacing Excel tracking of ~15 renewals/month), a shared 'purchasing user' role, richer contract reporting/analytics, automatic PO creation post-contract-execution, two/three-way invoice matching with PO-balance visibility, more user-friendly/editable supplier-intake questions, and Slack-based intake via Merlin (Avoma discovery call and demo, 14 and 16 Jan 2026). Overarching all of it is a gating requirement: bi-directional NetSuite integration, since BitSight is not evaluating an ERP change (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c). RFP responses formalizing these criteria were submitted 18-30 Mar 2026 (Next_Step_History__c).

win_position.text: Champion Amy Snider ran BitSight through a full department-level evaluation (RFP submitted 18 Mar 2026; IT demo 21 Apr, Legal demo 17 Apr, Finance/AP demo 22 Apr 2026) and told the rep on 11 May 2026 she was 'confident in the demos... and expecting to communicate results/next steps that week.'

Zycus is well-positioned on BitSight's stated gating requirement, bi-directional Oracle NetSuite integration, via native connectors and open APIs (Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c), directly addressing a factor the buyer has flagged as non-negotiable since it is not replacing its ERP (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c).

No economic buyer or executive sponsor is mapped (Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c = false); the relationship runs through only 2 opportunity contact roles (Amy Snider and Bernardo Silva), and there is no Zycus line to the new CEO now cited as blocking the decision (Next Step, 6 Jul 2026).
The deal has held in Shortlisted since 10 Mar 2026 (121 days as of 9 Jul 2026), well past typical stage dwell, with Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c still false and no paper process started.

deal_momentum.text: The last confirmed two-way buyer engagement was Amy Snider's email on 11 May 2026 (~59 days ago); every Next Step update since (21 May, 28 May x2, 11 Jun, 17 Jun, 29 Jun, 6 Jul 2026) is a rep-authored plan note, not a buyer reply.
No dated future meeting or milestone is currently scheduled with BitSight.
The rep has kept working the account rather than going silent, sharpening the pitch from a generic check-in to a targeted GEP service/support differentiation call.

customer_commitment.text: Amy Snider is named champion (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c) and has volunteered detailed requirements and process information across both fully-read Avoma sessions plus one logged email; her 11 May 2026 message is the strongest positive buyer signal on record, if now dated.

Bernardo Silva (Accounts Payable Specialist) attended only the 16 Jan 2026 demo and has not been the subject of any further engagement; no other BitSight stakeholder has been re-engaged since the April department demos.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 65-minute 1/16/2026 demo, BitSight produced 6 action items. The single AI-adjacent item is Amy Snider's internal-facing commitment to check with the legal team whether they currently use a CLM platform or AI-driven contract management. That is an internal inquiry, not a Zycus AI requirement, and pairs with BitSight's self-rated AI need on the RFP at the lowest non-zero tier, Basic Automation, rule based.
• The other action items are demo logistics: Ritika to open contract dashboards for visibility, Amy to review all vendor demos this month and shortlist by the third week of February, and Michael McCarthy to prepare a next demo scripted around the customer's actual contract data. The evaluation is being driven by demo quality and dashboard visibility, not AI capability.
• The most recent 5/11/2026 rep note records Global Director of Procurement Amy Snider confirming confidence in demos seen so far, with selection driven by demo quality. Products in scope are iContract, iSupplier, eInvoicing, and Merlin only on the AI side. Transcript keyword review confirmed no additional buyer-voiced AI engagement beyond the legal-internal-check, lifting confidence to High.
